title: Line continuation characters '\' should not be followed by trailing whitespace
description: Line continuation characters '\' should not be followed by trailing whitespaceAnalyze your codeintentionality-clearreliabilityBugWhy is this an issue?More InfoWhen a line of code ends with the\(backslash) character, the newline character is deleted. The compiler considers the next line a continuation of the current line, resulting in only one logical line of code.This source code transformation, known asline-splicing, occurs during the second phase of C and C++ translation.Several compilers have a loose implementation ofline-splicingand allow whitespace after the\character. While this practice was non-portable until C++23, it remains non-portable in C.
recommendation: Review and fix according to the rule guidelines.

noncompliant:
#define MULTI_LINE_MACRO \   
    value1 + \	
    value2 // Noncompliant: spaces and tab after backslashes

#define FUNCTION(x, y) \  
    ((x) + (y)) // Noncompliant: trailing spaces after backslash

const char* longString = \	 
    "This is a long string"; // Noncompliant: tab and space after backslash
compliant:
#define MULTI_LINE_MACRO \
    value1 + \
    value2 // Compliant: no trailing whitespace after backslashes

#define FUNCTION(x, y) \
    ((x) + (y)) // Compliant: clean line continuation

const char* longString = \
    "This is a long string"; // Compliant: proper line continuation

checks.message: Line continuation characters '\' should not be followed by trailing whitespace
